title: rubus decumbens
intro: Rubus decumbens, also known as the creeping raspberry, is a type of bramble or thorny shrub native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Rubus decumbens include its potential to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and gastritis, as well as its use as an antiviral agent. Rubus decumbens is commonly prepared for herbal use in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in the production of jams and preserves.
